data.title: Sanlam Kenya Posts FY PBT Of 1.66 Billion KShs
data.description:
Sanlam Kenya Plc :

data.title: Pondy Oxides Forms New Limited Liability Partnership
data.description:
Pondy Oxides And Chemicals has established a limited liability partnership called POCL Recycle City LLP, to foray into the business of dismantling, shredding of ferrous and non-ferrous metal and other non-metallic scrap, according to a Wednesday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The LLP has been formed for recycling, responsible manufacturing practices, circular economy, and sustainability as core initiatives.

data.title: European Lithium Releases Greenland Rare Earth Project's Mineral Resource Estimate
data.description:
European Lithium reported a maiden mineral resource estimate of 45 million tonnes at 0.4% total rare earth oxides from the Tanbreez Project in Greenland, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

NASDAQ-listed Critical Metals completed an initial investment and stage 1 interest to hold a 42% stake in Tanbreez, after entering into a heads of agreement in June 2024 with Rimbal to acquire up to 92.5%.

Critical Metals completed due diligence and is preparing a report for filing with the US Securities and Exchange Commission. It has also undertaken a recent drilling program for confirmation, extension, and infill drilling.

European Lithium is Critical Metals's largest shareholder and as such now considers the project to be material. Together both firms hold a combined interest of 49.5% in the project.

European Lithium's shares rose 9% on market close on Thursday.

data.title: Eurobio Scientific to Buy Voden Medical's Life Science Unit in Italy
data.description:
Eurobio Scientific Société anonyme agreed to buy the life science unit of Voden Medical Instruments in Italy.

The in vitro specialty medical diagnostics and life sciences company said Wednesday the acquisition is part of its expansion in the country where it recently acquired Quimark SRL.

data.title: Pan United Completes Concrete Casting Activity on Singapore's Tallest Building
data.description:
Pan United through its subsidiary, Pan-United Concrete, concluded a mass pour of 10,250 cubic meters of concrete casting on the raft foundation of The Skywaters at 8 Shenton Way in January, according to a Wednesday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The mixed-use development in Singapore spans 10,984 square meters and will be the city-state's tallest building once completed in 2028, the filing said.

Through a collaboration with main contractor China Harbor Engineering Company, the mass pour was carried out 35 hours non-stop with a truckload of concrete delivered every two minutes.

data.title: EMEA Morning Briefing: Trade Worries Remain in Focus
data.description:
MARKET WRAPS

Watch For:

EU industrial production; trading updates from Hannover Re, Deliveroo, Enel, Hugo Boss, Halma, Generali

Opening Call:

European stock futures fell early Thursday. Asian stock benchmarks were mixed; the dollar and Treasury yields were little changed; while oil edged lower and gold futures gained.

Equities:

Stock futures point to a lower open Thursday as markets weighed tariff developments, following retaliatory measures by Canada and the EU against the U.S.

Overnight gains in U.S. indexes after a tame inflation report, assuming they hold, won't be enough to recover losses from earlier in the week, reflecting the market's unease.

"If this malaise persists for longer than the next few months, we will start to see the consumer and corporations pull back spending, and growth will subside," said Skyler Weinand, chief investment officer with Regan Capital.

-

Germany's draft budget plan, to be voted on in the next week, should revive German growth, which has been absent for years, BNP Paribas economists said. Proposals from the likely incoming governing coalition partners could generate almost 120 billion euros in additional annual expenditure.

"This spending would directly stimulate public consumption and investment and indirectly stimulate the economy as a whole." German growth could therefore reach 0.9% in 2025 and 2026, higher than BNP's current forecasts of 0.2% and 0.5% respectively, and 2 percentage points higher than currently expected in 2029, the bank said.

Forex:

"Elevated trade uncertainty has now become a negative for business confidence, for investment, and for growth," DBS's Chang Wei Liang said. The outlook for U.S. economic exceptionalism could be fading, he said. The dollar index is consolidating around the mid-103s, getting little boost from escalating trade concerns, he added.

Bonds:

Recent turbulence in financial markets is making corporate bonds look cheaper relative to Treasurys, but still not enough to compensate for the increased risk stemming from policy uncertainty tied to Trump, Madison Investments' Mike Sanders said.

"We have been reducing some relative credit risk in our portfolios," Sanders said. He is buying more mortgages and owns "a good amount" of Treasurys. "We are just kind of watching the market and seeing if there is an opportunity to add either mortgages or credit."

Energy:

Oil futures fell slightly early Thursday amid global tariff concerns. Uncertainty over tariffs, together with uncertain demand in China continues to put pressure on the energy market, Pepperstone's Quasar Elizundia said.

"These factors, combined with Canada's recent decision to impose tariffs exceeding $20 billion on U.S. products, add volatility to the international oil landscape," he added.

Metals:

Gold edged higher in Asia. The prospects of a global trade war could enhance the safe-haven appeal of the precious metal. There's uncertainty over the impact of tariffs on the U.S. economy amid talk of a recession, XS.com's Samer Hasn said in an email. Market turmoil has also deepened due to President Trump's repeated "start-and-stop" over tariffs, Hasn added.

-

Copper prices rose slightly in Asian trading. Citi thinks prices could remain strong into 2Q amid physical market tightness, excluding the U.S. Copper raw-material tightness will likely persist amid weak global mine supply growth and a likely reduction in U.S. copper scrap exports,the bank said.

"Price risks appear more balanced from April as re-emerging tariff headwinds offset continued higher US copper import demand, which we think can extend for much of 2Q," Citi said.

TODAY'S TOP HEADLINES

Trump set to nominate Michelle Bowman as Fed's top bank supervisor

President Donald Trump is set to nominate Federal Reserve Governor Michelle Bowman to be the central bank's top bank supervisor, a White House official confirmed to MarketWatch.

Bowman has been on the Fed board since Trump nominated her in 2018. She was the first board member appointed to a seat set aside by Congress to be filled by a representative of the community-banking industry.

Consumer Angst Is Striking All Income Levels

American consumers have had a lot to fret about so far this year, between never-ending tariff headlines, stubborn inflation and most recently, fresh fears about a recession. These concerns seem to be hitting spending by both rich and poor, across necessities and luxuries, all at once.

Take low-income consumers: At an interview at the Economic Club of Chicago in late February, Walmart Chief Executive Doug McMillon said "budget-pressured" customers are showing stressed behaviors: They are buying smaller pack sizes at the end of the month because their "money runs out before the month is gone." McDonald's said in its most recent earnings call that the fast-food industry has had a "sluggish start" to the year, in part because of weak demand from low-income consumers. Across the U.S. fast-food industry, sales to low-income guests were down by a double-digit percentage in the fourth quarter compared with a year earlier, according to McDonald's.

LVMH Taps Arnault's Son to Head Loro Piana in Leadership Reshuffle

PARIS-LVMH named Frédéric Arnault, son of chairman Bernard Arnault, as the new head of Italian fashion house Loro Piana, a move that bolsters the 30-year-old's credentials as a contender to one day succeed his father at the helm of the luxury conglomerate.

The move is a significant step for the younger Arnault, who has worked in LVMH's watch division since 2017, most recently overseeing a stable of brands that includes TAG Heuer, Hublot, and Zenith. At Loro Piana, he will trade high-performance chronographs for the rarefied world of $2,000 sweaters and hand-finished vicuña coats.

Niantic to Sell Pokemon GO, Other Games to Saudi-Backed Group in $3.5 Billion Deal

"Pokemon GO" maker Niantic reached an agreement to sell its gaming business to Savvy Games Group's subsidiary, Scopely, for $3.5 billion, handing the company backed by Saudi Arabia's sovereign wealth fund the hit mobile game along with engagement and live-experience apps.

"Pokemon GO," one of the first videogames to use augmented reality, exploded in popularity after launching in 2016. The game allows players use their smartphone cameras to find and capture virtual creatures. The franchise amassed more than $8 billion in revenue since its inception and the game reaches players in over 190 countries and regions, Savvy Games Group said.

Intel Names Lip-Bu Tan as Chief Executive Officer

Intel named Lip-Bu Tan as its new chief executive, selecting a former board member and longtime industry executive to help turn around the beleaguered semiconductor company.

Tan, the former CEO of Cadence Design Systems, is joining a onetime industry-leading semiconductor manufacturer that has lost market share and struggled to execute a strategy to keep up with other chip makers.

Write to singaporeeditors@dowjones.com

Expected Major Events for Thursday

00:01/UK: Feb RICS Residential Market Survey

07:00/ROM: Jan Industrial production

07:00/ROM: Feb CPI

07:00/SWE: Feb CPI

07:30/SWI: Feb Import Price Index

07:30/SWI: Feb PPI

08:00/CZE: Jan Retail trade

08:00/SVK: Jan Employment and average monthly wage in selected branches

08:00/SVK: Jan Turnover in selected branches of economy, incl Industry & Construction

09:00/ITA: 4Q Labour Cost Index

09:00/FRA: Mar IEA Oil Market Report

09:30/UK: 4Q Business Finance Review

10:00/EU: Jan Industrial Production

11:00/FRA: 4Q OECD Quarterly National Accounts G20 GDP growth

11:00/IRL: Feb CPI

All times in GMT. Powered by Onclusive and Dow Jones.

Write to us at newsletters@dowjones.com

We offer an enhanced version of this briefing that is optimized for viewing on mobile devices and sent directly to your email inbox. If you would like to sign up, please go to https://newsplus.wsj.com/subscriptions.

This article is a text version of a Wall Street Journal newsletter published earlier today.

data.title: Couche-Tard lobs ball back to Seven & i's court
data.description:
The Canadian side is getting serious about its $47 bln bid, holding a Tokyo press conference to address antitrust and other concerns. That adds pressure on incoming CEO Steven Dacus to seriously engage the suitor or face investor blowback as shares languish below the offer price.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow @KangHexin on X

CONTEXT NEWS

Alimentation Couche-Tard held its first Tokyo press conference on March 13 to lay out its case for a proposed $47 billion takeover of Japan’s Seven & i Holdings.

The Canadian company’s management said concerns over antitrust issues in the U.S. were overblown and suggested the Japanese company’s local stores could be carved out if necessary to address domestic concerns over national security. They also ruled out a listing for Seven & i's U.S. business, proposed by incoming CEO Steven Dacus as part of a broader plan to boost shareholder returns that includes a 2 trillion yen ($13.5 billion) share buyback.

Couche-Tard founder Alain Bouchard said his firm could potentially find areas of greater value between the two companies but as yet had received "virtually no access to information" from Seven & i.

Shares in Seven & i were trading at 2,196 yen per share following the press conference, about 18% below Couche-Tard’s offer price.

data.title: South Africa's Exxaro names veteran mining executive Ben Magara new CEO
data.description:
Exxaro Resources , a South African coal miner, said it has appointed veteran mining executive Ben Magara as its new CEO following the departure of Nombasa Tsengwa.

Magara is a former CEO of platinum miner Lonmin and has also worked for Anglo American.

The South African coal miner is naming a new boss to steer the company after its headline earnings per share fell 36% to 30.16 rand ($1.65) in 2024 due mainly to lower coal prices.

data.title: Outlook for record Chinese second-quarter soybean imports may ease supply tightness
data.description:
Key points:
  • Brazilian harvest delays tighten China's soybean supplies
  • Chinese processors suspend operations amid supply squeeze
  • China's soybean imports expected to rise from April

China is poised to receive record soybean imports in the second quarter, traders and analyst said, after delayed Brazilian shipments and slow customs clearances have caused supply tightness that forced several processors to halt operations.

The world's largest soybean buyer is forecast to import a record high of 31.3 million metric tons of the oilseed in the April to June period, alleviating supply pressure from smaller arrivals expected during March, according to the average of forecasts from five research and trading firms.

That is a roughly 4.6% rise from 29.91 million tons imported during the second quarter of last year, as freshly harvested beans from Brazil's bumper crop flow into China.

"South America soybean prices, particularly Brazilian new crop soybean, are more attractive than their counterpart, as such Chinese crushers have purchased rather large volumes of Brazilian new crop soybeans," said Cheang Kang Wei, assistant vice president at StoneX in Singapore.

China's recent supply squeeze stems from buyers avoiding U.S. beans amid concerns over a trade war with Washington, along with delays in Brazil's harvest, the world's biggest soybean producer.

Beijing retaliated last week against new U.S. tariffs by increasing duties on $21 billion worth of agricultural products, including soybeans.

"The soybean shortage during this period has been more widespread and severe, prompting a growing number of soybean mills across the country to halt operations," said Liu Jinlu, agricultural researcher at Guoyuan Futures.

MARCH IMPORTS TO DROP

March imports are expected to shrink to a five-year low of 5.27 million tons, according to StoneX.

Chinese firms imported a record 105 million tons of soybeans in 2024, but high crush rates to meet livestock feed demand and stockpiling ahead of U.S. President Donald Trump's January inauguration reduced inventories.

According to data from consultancy Mysteel, soybean inventory at Chinese ports shrank to 4 million tons by March 7, a decrease of 600,700 tons from the previous week and down from 892,500 tons from the same period last year.

"Soymeal stocks are pretty tight, which is reflected in prices but we expect the situation to ease from April," said one oilseed trader in Singapore. "Prices of soybean products will come under pressure."

The March soybean crush is forecast at 5.84 million tons, a 10.1% decline from February and a 19.1% from a year earlier, StoneX's Cheang said.

Tight supplies have driven crush margins in processing hub of Rizhao to more than 450 yuan ($62.19) per ton. (CNSOY-RZO-MRG)

A slower pace of customs clearance at Chinese ports added to the supply squeeze.

China has in recent years increased the rate of quality inspections on imported soybean cargoes, lengthening clearing times and delaying discharge amid rising imports of the oilseed. Some shipments from the port to crushing plants can take between 20 and 25 days, up from 15 days typically, said a China-based trader.

($1 = 7.2356 Chinese yuan renminbi)

data.title: Offshore Yuan Steady
data.description:
The offshore yuan steadied around 7.23 per dollar after reversing the four-month high reached in the previous session, as investors conitnued to asses the impact of escalating global trade war tensions.

During a briefing on Wednesday, China stated that it has successfully controlled drug trafficking and has done everything it can to assist the US, suggesting that the US should express gratitude rather than impose tariffs on Chinese imports.

Last week, President Trump raised tariffs on China, citing insufficient efforts to address the drug crisis and calling the flow of drugs into the U.S. 'an unusual and extraordinary threat.' Similarly, U.S. President Donald Trump's 25% tariffs on steel and aluminum took effect on Wednesday, targeting Canada, Australia, the EU, and other trading partners.

In response, the EU and Canada imposed retaliatory measures, while Australia chose not to enact counter-tariffs.

data.title: Austrian Construction Group Porr's Nominates New COO
data.description:
Porr (POS.VI, ) nominated Josef-Dieter Deix as its new chief operating officer, according to a Wednesday release.

Deix will succeed Jürgen Raschendorfer after the Austrian construction company's supervisory board votes on the nomination on March 26.

data.title: Spinnova's CEO to Step Down in September
data.description:
Spinnova's Chief Executive Officer Tuomas Oijala plans to step down, effective Sept. 12, following a six-month notice period.

The Finnish textile fiber and materials manufacturer commenced the search process for Oijala's successor with immediate effect, according to a Wednesday release.

data.title: UBM Development to Launch EUR3 Million Share Buyback
data.description:
UBM Development (UBS.VI) said Wednesday it plans to launch a share buyback program of up to 3 million euros from March 18.

The Austrian timber construction group is looking to repurchase up to 747,218 shares or 10% of its share capital at 7 euros apiece under the scheme until Nov. 18.

data.title: Scatec Kicks Off Commercial Operations at Botswana Solar Project
data.description:
Scatec commenced commercial operations of the first 60 megawatts of a 120-megawatt solar project in Botswana.

The renewable energy company said Wednesday the Mmadinare Solar Cluster is its first in the country and has a 25-year power purchase agreement in place with national utility Botswana Power Corp.

Construction is underway on the remaining 60 megawatts and completion is expected in early 2026.

Elsight Ltd :

data.title: Douson Drilling & Production Equipment Invests 10 Million Yuan in Equity Fund; Shares Fall 3%
data.description:
Suzhou Douson Drilling & Production Equipment subsidiary Levi Investment will invest 10 million yuan to acquire a 22.2% stake in Gongqingcheng Bifang Venture Capital Partnership.

The fund focuses on equity investments in unlisted enterprises, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Shares of the wellhead equipment maker were down over 3% in recent trade.

data.title: City Developments Shares Rise After Executive Chairman Discontinues Lawsuit
data.description:
By Amanda Lee

City Developments' shares rose sharply Thursday, after the executive chairman of the Singapore-listed property developer said he would drop his lawsuit against his son and other board members.

Shares were recently 3.85% higher at 5.13 Singapore dollars, equivalent to US$3.85.

In late February, Kwek Leng Beng said that he had filed court papers to deal with what he called an "attempted coup" on the company.

The elder Kwek had said his son--Sherman Kwek--and a group of directors sought to consolidate control of the board and the group by changing the composition of the board and "hastily" making changes to the board committees and the governance of the group.

On the late Wednesday, the elder Kwek said that the legal action relating to the board resolutions would be discontinued.

The elder Kwek, said he will continue his role as executive chairman, while the younger Kwek will remain as group chief executive officer. All current directors will remain on the company's board, he added.

"All the Board members have agreed to put aside their differences for the greater good of CDL and its stakeholders," the elder Kwek said.

Write to Amanda Lee at amanda.lee@wsj.com

data.title: NRC Group Bags SEK238 Million Contract to Upgrade Railway Station in Sweden
data.description:
NRC Group said Wednesday its NRC Group Sverige subsidiary secured the 238 million-krona contract to upgrade the Nyköping railway station in Sweden.

Infrastructure group OHLA Sverige is commissioning the project.

The Norwegian rail infrastructure company said the project involves the provision of services to the track, electric and telecommunications lines of the railway. Works will start in March 2025, with completion scheduled for June 2027.

data.title: Hang Seng Index Extends Losses; Last 1.1% Lower at 23337.20
Poland's Allegro sees earnings rise 8-12% at home in 2025, sets share buyback
data.description:
Poland's biggest e-commerce platform Allegro expects its earnings to rise 8-12% at home this year, it said on Thursday while proposing an around 1.4 billion zloty ($363.95 million) share buyback.

Allegro's fourth-quarter adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortisation (EBITDA) were 975.2 million zlotys in the Polish market, compared with 960 million zlotys expected by analysts in a company-compiled consensus.

($1 = 3.8467 zlotys)

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Thursday at 1 AM ET
data.description:
Stocks Fluctuate on Muted Inflation Data, Loud Trade Fight

The S&P 500 and Nasdaq ended higher after investors weighed the latest trade skirmish with Canada and a solid inflation report. The Dow industrials edged down.

U.S. Federal Deficit Exceeds $1 Trillion Over 5 Months

Data disclosed by the Treasury Department showed an adjusted gap between federal spending and revenue of $1.063 trillion for October through February.

Trump set to nominate Michelle Bowman as Fed's top bank supervisor

President Donald Trump is set to nominate Federal Reserve Governor Michelle Bowman to be the central bank's top bank supervisor, a White House official confirmed to MarketWatch.

Consumer Angst Is Striking All Income Levels

Signs of weakness are showing up in spending on everything from basics to luxuries.

The Stock Market Is Finally Rallying. It May Be a Head Fake.

A tame inflation report is welcome news, but markets remain worried about President Donald Trump's tariffs and trade war.

Europe, Canada Hit Back at U.S. Steel Tariffs

The EU is targeting a range of American goods, including whiskey and motorcycles, with 50% tariffs, taking aim at politically sensitive products.

Inflation Cooled to 2.8% in February, Lower Than Expected

Inflation eased last month, but the data may offer less comfort to businesses, consumers and the Fed than it otherwise would as looming tariffs may keep prices rising.

Bank of Canada Cuts Policy Rate to 2.75% as Economy Faces 'New Crisis'

The central bank must balance the risk of tariff-fueled higher inflation and the damage to growth from U.S. trade policy, Gov. Tiff Macklem says.

WTO's Trade Barometer Picks Up, Pointing to Resilient Flows

The World Trade Organization's trade barometer rose, but it warned increased purchases by those seeking to get ahead of threatened tariffs might exaggerate the health of the global trading system.

Wind and Solar Overtake Coal Power for First Time in U.S.

The two renewable energy sources accounted for 17% of the country's power mix while coal fell to a low of 15%. Natural gas remains by far the largest source of electricity, accounting for 43%.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Thursday at 1 AM ET
data.description:
Intel Names Lip-Bu Tan as Chief Executive Officer

The former CEO of Cadence Design Systems, steps in as the semiconductor manufacturer has lost market share.

Couche-Tard Doubles Down on Friendly Takeover of 7-Eleven Owner

The Canadian owner of Circle K will continue its friendly pursuit of Seven & i, executives said, sounding confident that they can overcome antitrust issues that could block the prospective acquisition in the U.S.

Adobe Stock Slides Despite Earnings Beat. The CFO Is Confident About AI.

Adobe posted first-quarter adjusted earnings of $5.08 a share, beating estimates of $4.97 a share.

LVMH Taps Arnault's Son to Head Loro Piana in Leadership Reshuffle

The fashion role is expected to bolster Frédéric Arnault's credentials as a candidate to one day lead the luxury-goods giant.

Puma Will Cut 500 Positions Globally as Part of a Cost-Cutting Plan

Job reductions will be implemented at the group's headquarters in Germany and in regional offices. The company has a total workforce of 22,500.

D-Wave Claims 'Quantum Supremacy,' Beating Traditional Computers

D-Wave is the latest quantum computing company to say it has achieved "quantum supremacy," better known as the ability to solve a problem with a quantum computer that can't be done with a traditional computer.

Niantic to Sell Pokemon GO, Other Games to Saudi-Backed Group in $3.5 Billion Deal

Niantic is also selling "Pikmin Bloom," and "Monster Hunter Now," Niantic's most recent game.

Sunnova Prepares Restructuring Talks That Could Include Bankruptcy Filing

Sunnova Energy, one of the country's largest residential solar companies, is preparing to engage with creditors to explore options including a potential bankruptcy filing over its roughly $8.5 billion in debt, according to people familiar with the matter.

Wonder Acquires Tastemade for Its Latest Quest: to Make a 'Mealtime Super App'

Wonder CEO Marc Lore hopes the company will one day offer AI-powered autonomous food ordering for all three meals of the day.

Whiskey Is a Target of Europe's Retaliatory Tariffs. Brown-Forman Stock Slides.

The European Union plans to establish countermeasures in April that apply to products ranging from boats to bourbon.

data.title: Hang Seng Tech Index Extends Losses; Last 2.3% Lower at 5709.30
Woodside Energy Faces Dissent from Advocacy Group over Directors' Elections
data.description:
Woodside Energy Group (ASX: WPL) is facing dissent from the Australasian Center for Corporate Responsibility (ACCR), which has called for a vote against all directors standing for either re-election or election, according to a Thursday statement by the ACCR.

The shareholder advocacy and research organization argues that despite chronically poor shareholder returns, the company continues to follow a high-cost, high-carbon, and low-value strategy.

The group also points to shareholder opposition to Woodside's Climate Transition Action Plan (CTAP), with 58% voting against the plan in 2024.

The board has failed to meaningfully respond to investor concerns on both governance and climate risk, ACCR said.

Woodside's total shareholder returns over the past 15 years have been 168% lower than the ASX100.

Woodside Energy did not immediately respond to a request for comment by MT Newswires.

data.title: Hongbo's Unit Cancels Cloud Service Deal With Baichuan Intelligent Tech; Shares Slump 5%
data.description:
Hongbo's unit, Beijing Yingbo Digital Technology, agreed to terminate a cloud service agreement with Beijing Baichuan Intelligent Technology, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on Thursday.

Under the agreement, which was signed in December 2023, Yingbo Digital Tech was supposed to provide Baichuan Intelligent with computing power, resources, and supporting software, applications, and technical services until Jan. 31, 2027.

The agreement was canceled due to unforeseen circumstances, the Chinese commercial paper printing company said, but the parties will separate without any disputes.

Hongbo's shares dropped 5% in recent trade.
